rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical iodosulfonylation/spirocyclization of 1,5-enyne-containing para-quinone methides with arylsulfonyl hydrazides and KI to give iodosulfonated spirocyclohexadienone-containing (E)-indenes.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (I-) to iodonium (I+) via loss of two electrons; I+ acts as redox mediator that oxidizes TsNHNH2 to generate arylsulfonyl iodide (Ts-I) which homolyzes to a sulfonyl radical and an iodine radical (re-oxidized to I+ at the anode). Cathode reduces phenoxyl radical intermediate B' to phenolate anion C and reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: iodonium cation (I+) generated by anodic oxidation of I-
cyclohexadienone carbon radical B (sulfonyl 1,6-addition adduct to p-QM)
phenol-type aromatic radical B' (zwitterionic resonance form of B)
phenolate anion C (cathodic reduction of B')
vinyl/alkenyl iodonium-alkyne adduct D (I+ added to alkyne of C)
diarylmethyl sulfone 4 (proposed key intermediate isolated by control)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes I- to I+ (2 e-), which oxidizes TsNHNH2 to Ts-I; homolysis gives Ts• that adds 1,6 to the p-QM alkene to form cyclohexadienone radical B, which tautomerizes to phenoxyl radical B'; the cathode reduces B' to phenolate C, then I+ activates the tethered alkyne and intramolecular 5-exo-dig ipso-cyclization furnishes the iodo-spiroindene 3a, with concomitant cathodic reduction of protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: no current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product 3a formed without electric current, confirming electrochemistry is essential.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Complete inhibition of reaction (0% 3a), supporting a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: diarylmethyl sulfone 4 (isolated intermediate) + KI under standard conditions
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3a (28%) plus recovered p-QM 1a (47%), implicating 4 as an on-pathway intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: intermediate 4 + TsNHNH2 + KI
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3a in 80% yield, confirming 4 as a key intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: intermediate 4 + NIS / NaOH (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3a in 71% yield, indicating that I+ (electrogenerated) activates the alkyne for nucleophilic cyclization.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of KI, 1a, 2a and mixtures
probe_reagents.observation: Only KI-containing solutions show an oxidation peak at 1.85 V vs Ag/AgCl; 1a and 2a are not oxidized in 0-3.0 V window, so KI is the species oxidized at the anode.

notes: KI plays a triple role: electrolyte, redox mediator/catalyst (I-/I+), and iodine source for product C-I bond. Cathodic half-reaction is proton reduction to H2 (paired electrolysis). The (E)-selectivity arises from a 5-exo-dig ipso-cyclization of intermediate D. n-Butyl and cyclopropyl alkynyl substrates (1q, 1r) failed, suggesting aryl stabilization of the vinyl cation/radical at the alkyne terminus is required.
